briefBio: A paramount chief of the Herero people in German South West Africa (now Namibia) who led his people in the Herero Wars against German colonial rule. He is a key figure in Namibian anti-colonial history.
A Congolese independence leader and the first democratically elected Prime Minister of the Republic of the Congo, he became a martyr for the pan-African movement.
The second President of Egypt, a leader of the Egyptian Revolution of 1952, known for his pan-Arab nationalism and the nationalization of the Suez Canal.
The democratically elected Prime Minister of Iran from 1951 until 1953, when his government was overthrown in a coup. He is best known for nationalizing the Iranian oil industry.
A religious leader and theologian from Nejd in central Arabia who founded the reformist movement often referred to as Wahhabism.
The founder of the Baháʼí Faith. He claimed to be the prophetic fulfillment of Bábism, a spiritual messenger for this age, and his teachings emphasize unity and peace.
The founder of Bábism and a central figure in the Baháʼí Faith. He claimed to be the promised Mahdi and was executed for heresy in Persia.
The founder and first king of Saudi Arabia. He reconquered his family's ancestral home of Riyadh in 1902 and united the different regions of the Arabian Peninsula into a single state.
